explanation.rule: The suffix -ive (and its variants -ative, -itive) is added to verbs or nouns to form adjectives meaning 'having a tendency to', 'having the quality of', or 'relating to'. These adjectives are common in formal and academic contexts.

groups.note: The spelling of the base may change: the final -e is often dropped (compete → competitive, persuade → persuasive); -ion is replaced (production → productive).

examples.definition: wanting strongly to win or be the best
successful in producing the intended result
able to produce original and imaginative ideas
providing encouragement or help to someone

wordForms.definition: wanting strongly to win or be the most successful
wordForms.example: The market has become extremely competitive in recent years.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to try to win or be better than others

wordForms.definition: having the ability to produce original and imaginative ideas
wordForms.example: She has a very creative approach to problem solving.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to make or produce something new or original

wordForms.definition: successful in producing the intended result
wordForms.example: Exercise is an effective way to reduce stress.
wordForms.rootDefinition: the result or impact produced by an action

wordForms.definition: evoking admiration through size, quality, or skill
wordForms.example: The team delivered an impressive performance in the final.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to make someone admire or respect you

wordForms.definition: good at convincing others to do or believe something
wordForms.example: Her argument was so persuasive that the committee changed their vote.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to cause someone to believe or do something through reasoning

wordForms.definition: achieving a significant amount of results or output
wordForms.example: The meeting was productive and led to several new ideas.

wordForms.definition: reacting quickly and positively to requests or situations
wordForms.example: The customer service team is very responsive to complaints.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to reply or react to something

wordForms.definition: quick to detect or respond to feelings and situations
wordForms.example: You need to be sensitive when dealing with personal matters.
wordForms.rootDefinition: an awareness or feeling of something

wordForms.example: Her friends were very supportive during the difficult period.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to give help or encouragement to someone

wordForms.definition: willing to work together with others towards a shared goal
wordForms.example: A cooperative approach leads to better results for everyone.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to work together towards a common goal

multipleChoiceExercises.sentence: The new marketing strategy proved highly ___ in boosting sales.
She gave such a ___ speech that the committee changed their minds.
The athletes train hard to stay ___ at the international level.
The manager was very ___ of her team's efforts during the busy period.
The ___ use of colour in the design made it stand out immediately.
We need a ___ partner who will contribute equally to the project.

transformExercises.sentence: The view from the hilltop was absolutely ___. (IMPRESS)
The team had a very ___ afternoon, completing three major tasks. (PRODUCE)
Good teachers are ___ to their students' individual needs. (RESPOND)
It is important to be ___ when discussing someone's personal problems. (SENSE)
Working in a ___ environment encourages people to share ideas freely. (COOPERATE)
